qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: BinaryFormatter deserialization enabled by default allows arbitrary code execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns BinaryFormatter unsafe deserialization in System.Runtime.Serialization.Formatters, while the advisory concerns a System.Security.Cryptography.Cose security-check bypass in COSE payload handling.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described System.Security.Cryptography.Cose vulnerable code or root cause. The only qualifying finding is an open-world BinaryFormatter deserialization issue in a different package and vulnerable code path.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-qvhc-9v3j-5rfw affects NuGet package System.Security.Cryptography.Cose and describes malicious COSE payloads bypassing security checks, potentially causing unauthorized access or data manipulation.
Finding binaryformatter_deserialization_rce targets BinaryFormatter.Deserialize and BinaryObjectReader.FastBindToType in System.Runtime.Serialization.Formatters.
The finding evidence cites BinaryFormatter.Core.cs and BinaryObjectReader.cs, not COSE verification, COSE payload validation, or System.Security.Cryptography.Cose security checks.
